content.problemStatement:
We all know Gru loves Agnes very much. One day Agnes asked Gru to answer some of her queries. She lined up NNN minions in a straight line from 111 to NNN.

You are given an array AAA which contains the height of minions. Agnes will ask him several queries. In each query, Gru has to tell whether the bitwise AND of  A[L…R]A[L \ldots R]A[L…R] is EVEN or ODD. Since Gru is busy planning the biggest heist on Earth, he asks for your help.

###Input:


First line of the input contains an integer TTT denoting the number of test cases.
For each test case:-
First line contains an integer NNN denoting the number of elements.
Second line contains NNN spaced integer representing array elements.
Third line contains QQQ representing number of query.
Next QQQ lines contains two integer LLL and RRR as defined above.
###Output:
For each query, output "EVEN" or "ODD" without quotes.

###Constraints


1≤T≤101 \leq T \leq 101≤T≤10
1≤N≤1051 \leq N \leq 10^51≤N≤105
1≤Ai≤1051 \leq A_i \leq 10^51≤Ai​≤105
1≤Q≤1051 \leq Q \leq 10^51≤Q≤105
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T. Each test case contains an integer N, followed by N space-separated integers representing the array elements. The next line contains an integer Q, followed by Q queries, each containing two space-separated integers L and R.
content.outputFormat: For each query, output 'EVEN' or 'ODD' without quotes.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
For the first query, the bitwise AND of 1 and 3 is 1, which is Odd. Hence the first output is ODD.
For the third query, the bitwise AND of 2 and 4 is 0, which is Even. Hence the third output is EVEN.

Explanation:
For the first query, the bitwise AND of 1 and 2 is 2, which is Even. Hence the first output is EVEN.
For the third query, the bitwise AND of 3 and 4 is 4, which is Even. Hence the third output is EVEN.
For the fifth query, the bitwise AND of 1 and 10 is 2, which is Even. Hence the fifth output is EVEN.

Explanation:
For the first query, the bitwise AND of 1 and 2 is 2, which is Even. Hence the first output is EVEN.
For the third query, the bitwise AND of 3 and 4 is 4, which is Even. Hence the third output is EVEN.
For the sixth query, the bitwise AND of 1 and 15 is 1, which is Odd. Hence the sixth output is ODD.
For the eighth query, the bitwise AND of 5 and 10 is 2, which is Even. Hence the eighth output is EVEN.

solution.approach: Precompute the bitwise AND of all possible ranges of the array elements. Use a segment tree or a prefix sum array to efficiently compute the bitwise AND of any range.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N log N) for precomputation, O(log N) per query
